senses.definition: plural of senary, referring to a group of six or relating to the number six in a mathematical or classificatory context
examples: The dataset was analyzed in senaries to compare six groups at a time.
In some counting systems, senaries help organize data by six-value units.

senses.definition: plural of senarius, an ancient Roman six-man unit or a line of six in classical contexts
examples: The senarii were part of early Roman military or administrative organization.
In theater, senarii could refer to a line of six performers in a structure.

senses.definition: a Latin term meaning a group or unit of six; used in historical or scholarly contexts
examples: The manuscript mentions a senarius in a military arrangement.
Scholars discuss the senarius as a structural unit in Latin texts.

senses.definition: a mineral species (also known as arsenite of manganese or some related mineral) with a specific chemical composition
examples: The mineral catalog listed senarmontite among the arsenate minerals.
Geologists identified senarmontite in the ore sample.

senses.definition: plural of senarmontite, minerals occurring as a group or collection
examples: The cabinet displayed several senarmontites alongside other minerals, including senarmontite specimens.

senses.definition: relating to the number six; consisting of six parts or units
examples: The senary division of the chart groups data into six equal parts.
A senary numeral system would use base six.

senses.definition: ambiguous plural form; may appear as a misspelling or scarce usage of a regional term
examples: The word senas appeared in the text, but its meaning was unclear.
Consulting a lexicon helped determine if senas was a recognized term.

senses.definition: a formal group of representatives or lawmakers; the upper house of a legislature in some countries
examples: The senate debated the bill for hours.
She serves on the senate committee on finance.

senses.definition: plural of senate; multiple legislative bodies or sessions referred to as senates
examples: Several senates around the country passed new regulations.
The researchers compared decisions across different senates.

senses.definition: a member of a senate, a legislator
examples: The senator spoke about healthcare reform.
She hopes to become a senator in the future.

senses.definition: relating to a senator or to the senate; characteristic of a senator
examples: Senatorial duties include committee meetings and public hearings.
The senatorial election campaigns dominated the news cycle.

senses.definition: in a manner typical of a senator; with the dignity or rhetoric of a senator
examples: He spoke senatorially, with measured cadence and authority.
The proposal was presented senatorially, appealing to tradition.

senses.definition: relating to senators or a senate; classical or formal usage
examples: The senatorian code guided discussions in the chamber.
A senatorian tradition influenced the ceremonial aspects.

senses.definition: plural of senator; more than one member of a senate
examples: Several senators spoke during the debate.
The group of senators gathered for the vote.

senses.definition: the position, term, or office of a senator; the period of being a senator
examples: Her senatorship lasted for six years.
Campaign finance shaped his path to senatorship.

senses.definition: plural of senatorship; multiple terms or offices of senators
examples: Several regions experienced changes in senatorships after reform.
Historic records list several senatorships over the century.

senses.definition: pertaining to a senate or its members; relating to senators
examples: The senatory chamber convened for the irregular session.
Senatory duties include oversight and debate.

senses.definition: a female member of a senate; a feminine term for senator
examples: The senatress spoke with clear authority.
Historical texts mention several early senatresses.

senses.definition: plural of senatrix; a female leader or high-ranking woman of the senate or a council
examples: The senatrices advised on ceremonial procedures.
In some contexts, senatrices held influential advisory roles.

senses.definition: a female leader or high-ranking woman in a senate or council; a female senator
examples: The senatrix spoke at length about policy reform.
Records show the senatrix played a key role historically.

senses.definition: the Latin term for the Roman senate; also used in scholarly contexts to refer to the body itself
examples: The Roman senatus consulus held great authority.
Texts from antiquity discuss procedures of the senatus.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonstandard form; no established sense in standard English
examples: The author may have meant 'sense' or a proper noun; check the text.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonstandard form; no established sense in standard English
examples: No standard meaning; verify in context.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonstandard form; no established sense in standard English
examples: Context needed to determine meaning.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonstandard form; no established sense in standard English
examples: No clear sense without context.
